input.user_input: Add a force majeure clause to this supply agreement. Use the ISDA standard definition as a starting point.

actual:
It appears you didn't attach the supply agreement to your message. 

However, I have drafted a Force Majeure clause for you below, using the core concepts from the ISDA Master Agreement's standard definition (specifically, the focus on impossibility/impracticability, events beyond reasonable control, and the "all reasonable efforts" standard that does not require incurring a material loss to overcome the event). 

You can copy and paste this into your agreement, or if you upload the document, I can insert it for you.

***

**[X]. Force Majeure**

**(a) Definition of Force Majeure Event.** For the purposes of this Agreement, a "Force Majeure Event" means any event or circumstance (including, without limitation, any act of State or act of God) that prevents a party from performing its obligations under this Agreement, or makes it impossible or impracticable for a party to perform its obligations under this Agreement, provided that:
(i) such event or circumstance is beyond the reasonable control of the affected party; and 
(ii) the affected party could not, after using all reasonable efforts (which will not require such party to incur a loss, other than reasonable, immaterial, incidental expenses), overcome such prevention, impossibility, or impracticability.

**(b) Effect of Force Majeure Event.** If a Force Majeure Event occurs, the affected party will be excused from the performance of its obligations under this Agreement (other than any obligation to make a payment when due) to the extent and for the duration that such performance is prevented, made impossible, or made impracticable by the Force Majeure
